title:
Lesson 1:
Better / Better Not
description: 'Mejor' = better. 'Mucho mejor' = much better. 'Mejor sí' = it's better to do it / yes, let's. 'Mejor no' = better not / let's not. Very useful for expressing preferences and changing plans.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do we go out tonight or tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: ¿Y si vamos a cenar? ¿Mejor sí o mejor no?
dialogue.english: What if we go for dinner? Better yes or better no?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Mejor sí. La comida en casa está mucho mejor hoy.
dialogue.english: Better yes. The food at home is much better today.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: You're right. Much better to stay home.
